name: Joe Garland
abstract: Joseph Copeland Garland (August 15, 1903, Norfolk, Virginia – April 21, 1977, Teaneck, New Jersey) was an American jazz saxophonist, composer, and arranger, best known for writing "In the Mood". Garland studied music at Shaw University and the Aeolian Conservatory.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Garland's going to take over the band... I learned my lesson from the Blue Rhythm Band... So anyway, Joe Garland, he was the boss from then on.

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: And Joe Garland. And he also had Al Sears in the band... So he sent for me... Russell Procope... Joe Garland and Al Sears.

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Garland was on the band... I played with Claude up there.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Garland, he helped push it and them when I was on Cash Box--I mean Bandstand, he helped get them in. You see, I just went up there myself and Joe Garland helped get the men together.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: I had started writing then, with Joe Garland and Rudy Powell and all those guys in the band. They just taught me -- ... Like when Joe Garland used to give me the trumpet parts

about_quotes.speaker: Al Vollmer
about_quotes.evidence: Eventually I even got perhaps the most original recording which was the Edgar Hayes recording because that had Joe Garland in it, and Joe Garland wrote the tune.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Vollmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, June 8, 2017
